subtitle: Gravity is Optional After Midnight
location_text: Simon's Apartment, LA — 1:47 AM. Natalie's vigil.

ciphers.text: Natalie at the kitchen counter — not hanging, but suspended in the same vertical the card's tradition demands
ciphers.encoding_hint: the classical Hanged Man is suspended by one foot — the forced posture of surrender. Modern Mythology's Hanged Man is Natalie at 1:47 AM in someone else's apartment — the same suspension by different gravity. The chapter's premise is that midnight has already passed and the body has not chosen what comes next. The Hermit (IX) chose this stillness; Natalie was put in it.

ciphers.text: the maroon-backed tarot deck on the counter — face-down, full deck, cut once
ciphers.encoding_hint: Natalie carries the deck. The cut means she has drawn but not turned. The maroon back is the deck's only red — Lovers' tarnished gold goes red here. The deck-within-the-deck is the chapter's mirror: a Hanged Man card looks at her looking at a Hanged Man card.

ciphers.text: a beeswax candle — single wick, lit, burned to about half
ciphers.encoding_hint: ONE wick: the inventory specified 'Single Wick — Active.' A single wick is the Hermit's lantern brought indoors. The candle is the chapter's clock — the wax line marks how long she has waited. Half-burned means the chapter started before midnight and the player arrived in the middle.

ciphers.text: Natalie's notebook — open to a page of sigils, none finished
ciphers.encoding_hint: the notebook is Natalie's working journal. The sigils she draws here become legible on the Moon card (XVIII) — same hand, different chapter. XII → XVIII is the Hanged Man's preparation for the Moon's reading; she draws unfinished sigils now, will trace them through static later.

the phone log is the chapter's chronology. Natalie has been calling. Simon has not answered. The incoming unknown number at 23:47 is left as a hook — possibly Quentin's booth call from the Hierophant card, possibly Elicia, possibly the deck itself. The Hanged Man chapter is what happens when someone refuses to pick up.

ciphers.text: an apartment key on a ring with a small narwhal charm
ciphers.encoding_hint: the key is Natalie's permission to be here; the narwhal charm is the water-fauna through-line. The Hanged Man chapter's narwhal is on a keyring — the smallest scale the creature appears at. From Justice's mug (XI) to the Hanged Man's keychain (XII) the narwhal shrinks; from XIII Death onward it disappears.

ciphers.text: Natalie's shadow falls downward in candlelight — the Hermit's fell upward
ciphers.encoding_hint: IX ↔ XII mirror: same vertical, opposite direction. Hermit's shadow climbed; Hanged Man's falls. The deck's gentlest opposition is rendered as two figures alone at the same hour with the same dark, lit differently — one chose, one was chosen for. Natalie's vigil at 1:47 AM is the Hermit's solitude with the choice removed.

hotspots.notes: DORMANT: appears only after the player has played Quentin's voicemail on the Chariot. The 23:47 incoming-unknown line on Natalie's phone log is now annotated: 'origin: ember.ash.rest.bbs booth_caller.' Quentin called Natalie too. The St. Jude's booth dialed in three directions that night: Antonio (the Charioteer) picked up; Natalie did not; the third recipient is still unaccounted for. V → VII → XII is the deck's call chain; Natalie's refusal to answer is the Hanged Man chapter.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has noticed the upward-falling shadow on the Hermit card. The downward shadow is now visibly the Hermit's shadow inverted — same proportions, same diffusion, opposite direction. The IX ↔ XII mirror is the deck's gentlest opposition; the two cards complete each other once both shadows are seen.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read the completed sigils in the Moon's static field. The notebook on the Hanged Man counter now visibly shows the three sigils not just unfinished — but waiting. The pages are uncreased; the ink is fresh; the chapter knows its own completion happens six chapters later. XII → XVIII is Natalie's whole arc: the writing waits, the static reads it back.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read the Bed 4 walkout report in the Death (XIII) Ward C diorama. The empty stool across the kitchen counter from Natalie now visibly has a hospital armband on it — Mr. S., signed out of Ward C at 03:55 AMA, four hours after Natalie sat down at this counter. He asked the nurse at 03:30 whether the candle had gone out; Natalie's voicemail-two said the candle was two-thirds at 00:12. The candle in two registers, the same candle, the deck has been routing them past each other for two chapters.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has played Call 2 (no answer) in the Hierophant booth diorama. The 23:47 PT incoming-decline line on Natalie's phone log now visibly carries an annotation in fine ink: 'origin: st_judes.bbs vestibule booth — 06:38 CT, no zone-shifted note left.' The unknown caller was Father Quent. Natalie did not answer because she did not recognize the number; the church number she would not have recognized anyway. The booth-call chain V → XII closes from both sides.

ENTRY HOTSPOT: clicking the phone log on the painted card dispatches daemon_courier.12 to fetch the three outbound voicemails Natalie left for Simon. Each message has text-scroll playback; the third surfaces the chapter's deepest cruelty (Simon lit the candle before he left). The phone log on the painted card was already the OUTBOUND record; the diorama surfaces the audio. Three messages, no replies.

DORMANT: appears only after the World's wreath-gap hotspot fires. Natalie's notebook page now visibly carries a fourth sigil at the bottom margin, smaller than the three the painted card shows, drawn in a child's hand much older than the surrounding pages. The sigil is the Sinkhole's geological signature rendered as a teenager's tarot iconography. Natalie was approximately 12 at the Nexus event; she sketched the sigil that day and has kept the notebook for two decades. The chapter at XII is the chapter of Natalie carrying the original sigil into Simon's apartment in a notebook she will not let out of her sight. The Hanged Man's whole suspension register includes the suspension of a 12-year-old waiting for someone to come back from a hole in the ground. The chapter does not name what she was waiting for; the sigil holds the shape of the wait.

Vol6 forward seed: Natalie's family was at the Sinkhole. (Reserved: who was lost; whether Natalie's relationship to the D'Ambrosios is via Antonio's brief youth or via Marta's household or via a different inheritance.)

DORMANT: appears only after the Empress Aria-log diorama surfaces the bathroom-as-fallback evaluation. Natalie's apartment doorway now visibly shows a faint emerald HUD overlay along its frame — Aria's geometry, the same fallback Aria registered for Nicola, mapped onto Natalie's space. The Empress chapter's posture (alone in a chamber with the bathroom one room away as registered exit) IS the Hanged Man chapter's posture, six months later, on a different POV. III ↔ XII rendered as a shared body-architecture across two characters.

DORMANT: appears only after the Priestess pomegranate-hour shelf has been unlocked. The card now visibly carries a small burgundy VHS-spine label in the corner, marked "XII — THE HANGED MAN · 'the vigil'". Clicking it opens Elicia's relationship to this arcana as expressed in her episode for it. Elicia's relationship to vigil is the relationship of a person who has held many. The Hanged Man episode is her own all-night reading restaged for Natalie's hands. The three cards that repeat (Hanged Man, Moon, Star) are the three cards Elicia drew at her own vigil years earlier and could not shuffle out of. Natalie does not know Elicia held the vigil first. Natalie's comment under the episode — 'i did this. i still do this.' — is the deck's quietest acknowledgment that two women on different schedules have been holding the same vigil. The deck's XII chapter is partly Natalie's version. The episode is partly Elicia's.

easter_eggs: Natalie's vigil is the Hermit's solitude with the choice removed. IX → XII is the deck's spine on the topic of being alone — chosen, then assigned.
the narwhal through-line scales down: mug (XI) → keychain (XII) → absent (XIII onward). Faith carries the dog through dry stations, the narwhal carries through wet, and both stop at Death.

the maroon-backed deck is the only red in the deck's color tradition; Lovers' tarnished gold goes red here. Natalie carries a tarot deck inside the deck itself — the chapter's recursion is the smaller deck's cut card.
the unknown-number call at 23:47 is the third branch of Quentin's booth-call chain. Antonio picked up and died; Natalie didn't pick up and waits; the third recipient hasn't been named yet.

the Hanged Man chapter is also the chapter where Natalie's sigil-drawing begins — the same hand will read sigils in static on the Moon card (XVIII). XII prepares XVIII; the deck's longest single-character thread runs through these two cards.
